cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out the best title for this passage. The question is asking which option (A to D) is the most appropriate title. First, I'll read through the passage again to understand the main points. The article starts by talking about the London Olympics and how it's considered a success, especially in terms of being the "greenest" Games ever. It mentions that previous Olympics were criticized for environmental damage, but London aimed for a "One Planet Olympics" in their bid seven years prior. The next paragraphs discuss various green initiatives: cleaning up polluted areas, reusing 99% of debris, using recycled materials in construction like the steel tubes for the stadium roof. There's also info on green building techniques, water conservation through rainwater and recycled wastewater, and reducing waste by recycling or converting it to energy. They also promoted public transport to reduce carbon emissions from cars. Finally, the anti-doping efforts are mentioned as part of the "greenness." Now, looking at the options: A) How to deal with the anti-doping – The anti-doping part is mentioned towards the end, but it's a small part of the overall passage. The main focus isn't on anti-doping methods but how London made the Olympics green. So A is probably not the best choice. B) How to use recycled materials – While recycling is a significant part, the passage covers other aspects like construction techniques, water conservation, public transport, and even anti-doping. The title B is too narrow and doesn't cover all the points discussed. C) London's green games – This seems to encapsulate the overall theme. The entire passage is about how London made the Olympics environmentally friendly. It mentions multiple strategies (recycling, green building, water conservation, public transport, and even anti-doping as part of the green efforts). This title is broad enough to include all these points. D) London's public transport – Public transport is one of the aspects discussed, but again, it's just a part of the larger green initiatives.
